name: Salmon Rillettes
recipeIngredient: 3/4 lb Salmon, fillets or steaks, fresh, trimmed, skin on
Salmon, smoked, 3/4 lb, trimmed, and cut into shreds
Bread, white, loaf, sliced, 3/4-inch thick, crusts trimmed, each slice cut into 3 long strips
recipeInstructions:
See recipe for Court Bouillon.
1. Bring the Court Bouillon to a boil in a skillet.
2. Add the salmon and lower the heat to simmer. Place a sheet of buttered parchment paper or aluminum foil, buttered side down, over the salmon. Poach until the fish is just opaque in the center (about 8 minutes) or about 10 minutes per inch of thickness; do not overcook. Remove from the heat and cool salmon completely in the broth.
3. To make up a seasoning mixture, use a small spice or coffee mill to grind sea salt, 1/2 teaspoon peppercorns, and chili.
4. Drain the poached salmon, remove its skin, and cut it into 1/2-inch pieces. Place the salmon pieces, butter, Armagnac, and a pinch of the seasoning mixture into a processor and blend until very smooth. Add shredded smoked salmon to the processor and process with rapid pulses just until blended; there should be visible shreds of smoked salmon. Transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl.
5. Gently fold salmon roe into the mixture until blended, taking care not to crush the eggs. Adjust seasoning. Spoon mixture into a 4-cup souffle dish, straight-sided gratin dish, or casserole, lightly tapping mold on towel-lined work surface to eliminate any air bubbles. Cover with plastic wrap and chill. The Court Bouillon can be prepared up to 1-2 days ahead.
6. For Toast Strips:
- Preheat oven to 325°F. Rub a baking sheet with a thin film of olive oil. Arrange bread strips on the baking sheet and bake them in the center rack of the oven without turning until lightly golden (15-20 minutes).
- Remove rillettes from refrigerator 20 minutes before serving. Dip serving spoon in cold water and scoop onto cool plates. Serve with warm toast strips.
